intro: Pseuderanthemum teijsmannii, also known as the Java or Java false sage, is a tropical plant species native to Southeast Asia, belonging to the family Acanthaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, and its extracts have been found to possess several health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiparasitic properties. The bioactive constituents of Pseuderanthemum teijsmannii include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
